name: The Windows Firewall Driver failed to start
definition: Windows Security Event ID 5035 records that the Windows Firewall Driver failed to start or unexpectedly terminated.
trigger_scenarios: Windows logs this event when the Windows Firewall driver fails to start or unexpectedly terminates and includes an Error Code in the message text.

key_fields.explanation: The Microsoft schema exposes Error Code:%1 as the concrete driver-failure reason. Preserve the exact value from the event.
Identifies the host where packet filtering may be impaired before or during service startup.
Event 5035 is firewall-driver specific; correlate it with 5030 for service failure and boot/service events.

false_positives: Microsoft notes software or operating-system issues can cause this event.
Driver corruption or dependency failures during maintenance can create non-malicious failures.

attck_mapping.technique_name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify System Firewall

detection_notes: T1562.004 Disable or Modify System Firewall is supported when threshold=1 Event 5035 follows unauthorized driver, service, or firewall-policy modification. Retain the rendered ErrorCode from Error Code:%1 and correlate 5030, 5038, boot-time driver telemetry, and change records; no universal numeric error code is defined by the event reference.
kql_snippet:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 5035
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Event ID 5035
queries.query:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 5035
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, EventData
| order by TimeGenerated desc
assumptions: The Windows Security Events connector populates SecurityEvent.
Apply the event-specific field and correlation guidance on this page after retrieving the base events.

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Event ID 5035
queries.query:
index=wineventlog source="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=5035
| table _time, host, user, EventCode, Message
assumptions: Index, source, and field aliases vary by Splunk deployment and Windows add-on version.
Map provider fields from the raw event before adding event-specific filtering or aggregation.

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Event ID 5035
assumptions: The Windows Security channel is ingested into Cortex XDR.
Inspect raw provider fields because parsed names vary by collection path.

queries.title: Windows Security Event ID 5035
queries.query:
title: Windows Security Event ID 5035
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: security
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 5035
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative or system activity
level: informational
assumptions: This rule retrieves the event and is not a standalone malicious-behavior detection.
Add the field conditions and correlations documented on this page in the destination SIEM.

faqs.question: What does Windows Event ID 5035 mean?
faqs.answer: Event ID 5035 records that the Windows Firewall driver failed to start and includes a rendered error code.

faqs.question: Why is a firewall-driver failure high impact?
faqs.answer: The driver enforces network filtering below the service layer, so failure can reduce host firewall protection even when caused by a nonmalicious system problem.

faqs.question: Which telemetry should be correlated with 5035?
faqs.answer: Review 5030, driver and code-integrity events, service configuration, boot diagnostics, and recent security-product or operating-system changes.

sources.title: 5035: Microsoft Windows security auditing event reference
Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify System Firewall (T1562.004)
